---
title: "Part III --- Assessment and Feedback: Principles and Practice "
bibliography: references.bib
author:
  - name: TODO
    affiliations: TODO
  - name: TODO
    affiliations: TODO
---

SOME TEXT INTRODUCITNG THIS PART

## Theme --- Frameworks for assessment and feedback  

### TitleOfChapterPossiblyShortened [@TeachProgAcross_C38]

SOME TEXT INTRODUCITNG THIS CHAPTER [C38] Developing a skills-based framework for assessments in programming courses 

SOME TEXT EXPLAINING WHY THIS CHAPTER COULD BE FOR YOU

### TitleOfChapterPossiblyShortened [@TeachProgAcross_C35]

SOME TEXT INTRODUCITNG THIS CHAPTER [C35] Managing the Rubik’s Cube of Assessment: The Action Feedback Protocol 

SOME TEXT EXPLAINING WHY THIS CHAPTER COULD BE FOR YOU

## Theme --- Practical methods for assessing programming 

### TitleOfChapterPossiblyShortened [@TeachProgAcross_C26.1]

SOME TEXT INTRODUCITNG THIS CHAPTER [C26.1] Assessment and marking in an introductory programming course for physics students 

SOME TEXT EXPLAINING WHY THIS CHAPTER COULD BE FOR YOU

### TitleOfChapterPossiblyShortened [@TeachProgAcross_C02]

SOME TEXT INTRODUCITNG THIS CHAPTER [C02] Generative AI in Assessments: Before, During, and After 

SOME TEXT EXPLAINING WHY THIS CHAPTER COULD BE FOR YOU


### TitleOfChapterPossiblyShortened [@TeachProgAcross_C20]

SOME TEXT INTRODUCITNG THIS CHAPTER [C20] The use of automated marking in programming courses to enhance learning 

SOME TEXT EXPLAINING WHY THIS CHAPTER COULD BE FOR YOU
::: {.content-visible when-format="html" unless-format="epub"}

## References {.unnumbered}

:::
